Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can’t tear, it tosses far.

Name: Seedot
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

Name: Kricketot
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its legs are short. Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.

Name: Kricketune
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: There is a village that hosts a contest based on the amazingly variable cries of this Pokémon.

Name: Shaymin-EX
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pansage
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

Name: Simisage
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

Name: Foongus
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

Name: Amoonguss
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this Ability. If you do, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a loyal nature, it will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

Name: Growlithe
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a loyal nature, it will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

Name: Arcanine
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Burned.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

Name: Moltres
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

Name: Pansear
Flavor-Text: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

Name: Simisear
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

Name: Darumaka
Flavor-Text: Darumaka’s droppings are hot, so people used to put them in their clothes to keep themselves warm.

Name: Litwick
Flavor-Text: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leaches off the life force of any who follow it.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It arrives near the moment of death and steals spirit from the body.

Name: Chandelure
Effects: Does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Being consumed in Chandelure’s flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

Name: Reshiram-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon and heal all damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.

Name: Starmie
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

Name: Lapras
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

Name: Panpour
Flavor-Text: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

Name: Simipour
Effects: Draw 3 cards.
If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

Name: Basculin
Effects: If, before this Pokémon does damage, the Defending Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Red and blue Basculin usually do not get along, but sometimes members of one school mingle with the other’s school.

Name: Vanillite
Flavor-Text: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

Name: Vanillish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

Name: Vanilluxe
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each [C] in the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

Name: Frillish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: They paralyze prey with poison, then drag them down to their lairs, five miles below the surface.

Name: Jellicent
Effects: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does the same amount of damage to the Defending Pokémon.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They propel themselves by expelling absorbed seawater from their bodies. Their favorite food is life energy.

Name: Cubchoo
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Belt attack’s base damage is 40.
Flavor-Text: Its nose is always running. It sniffs the snot back up because the mucus provides the raw material for its moves.

Name: Beartic
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

Name: Kyurem-EX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

Name: Shinx
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

Name: Blitzle
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

Name: Zebstrika
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Item cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The energy made in its cheeks’ electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

Name: Zekrom-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It was born when sludge in a dirty stream was exposed to the moon’s X-rays. It appears among filth.

Name: Muk
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: A toxic fluids seeps from its body. The fluid instantly kills plants and trees on contact.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

Name: Gardevoir
Ability-Effect: Each basic [P] Energy attached to your [P] Pokémon provides [P][P] Energy. You can’t apply more than 1 Psychic Mirage Ability at a time.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Flavor-Text: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

Name: Munna
Flavor-Text: It eats the dreams of people and Pokémon. When it eats a pleasant dream, it expels pink-colored mist.

Name: Musharna
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other card back on top of your deck.
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: With the mist from its forehead, it can create shapes of things from dreams it has eaten.

Name: Darmanitan
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When weakened in battle, it transforms into a stone statue. Then it sharpens its mind and fights on mentally.

Name: Elgyem
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent’s brain, causing unendurable headaches.

Name: Beheeyem
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Choose a card from there and put it on the bottom of your opponent’s deck.
The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It can manipulate an opponent’s memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A well-trained one can sense auras to identify and take in the feelings of creatures over half a mile away.

Name: Hippopotas
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around ten.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: You may move 1 Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It is surprisingly quick to anger. It holds its mouth agape as a display of its strength.

Name: Mienfoo
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: They has mastered elegant combos. As they concentrate, their battle moves become swifter and more precise.

Name: Mienshao
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Pokémon Tool cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They use the long fur on their arms as a whip to strike their opponents.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: A smart and sneaky Pokémon. A pair may work together to steal eggs by having one lure the parents away.

Name: Weavile
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon has no Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does nothing.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Evolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: Move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The sound of its grass flute makes its listeners uneasy. It lives deep in forests.

Name: Shiftry
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 ft/second that can level houses.

Name: Scraggy
Effects: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It immediately headbutts anyones that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is massively thick.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
If this Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can smash concrete blocks with its kicking attacks. The one with the biggest crest is the group leader.

Name: Bronzor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There are researchers who believe this Pokémon reflected like a mirror in the distant past.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: Damage can’t be healed from any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Damage counters can still be moved.)
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each card in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It brought rains by opening portals to another world. It was revered as a bringer of plentiful harvests.

Name: Ferroseed
Effects: This Pokémon does 60 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: Does 20 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its fine fur feels sublime to the touch. It can expand its body by inhaling air.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

Name: Persian
Effects: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: A very haughty Pokémon. Among fans, the size of the jewel in its forehead is a topic of much talk.

Name: Regigigas-EX
Effects: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pidove
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon live in cities. They are accustomed to people. Flocks often gather in parks and plazas.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

Name: Cinccino
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

Name: Cilan
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Exp. Share
Description: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, you may move 1 basic Energy card that was attached to that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Heavy Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of 3 or more, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Level Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with 90 HP or less, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Center
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may heal 20 damage from 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Skyarrow Bridge
Description: The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon in play is [C] less.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Prism Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy.If the Pokémon this card is attached to is a Basic Pokémon, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

Name: Shaymin-EX
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Reshiram-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kyurem-EX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zekrom-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Regigigas-EX
Effects: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Emboar
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [R] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: All Energy attached to this Pokémon are [D] Energy instead of their usual type.
Effects: Does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.
